channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AMD’s leaked Ryzen 7 9800X3D performance boost over the 7800X3D might be a reason for you to wait before your next CPU upgrade
item.description: A new leak reveals AMD’s Ryzen 9 9800X3D taking a gaming performance jump over the popular 7800X3D, but should you upgrade once it launches?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Michael Keaton: Trump, Musk don't respect supporters
item.description: Actor Michael Keaton said former President Trump and tech billionaire Elon Musk don’t respect their supporters, in a video posted to Instagram this week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: How AI can alleviate help desk workloads
item.description: The rising flood of trouble tickets is straining help desk operations. Yet many still rely on phone calls, outdated knowledge bases, and manual processes. Unsurprisingly, this is leading to staff frustration and burnout, dissatisfied end users and persistent security vulnerabilities.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Larry Magid: Summit at Oxford focuses on Generative AI regulations
item.description: Although there were expected disagreements, I was left with a strong sense of hope in some shared values
item.dc:creator: Larry Magid, The Mercury News

item.title: Marc Benioff rails against Microsoft’s copilot
item.description: After lashing out against Microsoft at the Dreamforce customer conference, Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff has taken another swipe at his competitor. “When you look at how Copilot has been delivered to customers, it’s disappointing,” Benioff wrote in a post on Twitter/X.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Not your father’s avatar: The real future of artificial intelligence
item.description: Generative artificial intelligence (genAI) is the latest milestone in the “AAA” journey, which began with the automation of the mundane, lead to augmentation — mostly machine-driven but lately also expanding into human augmentation — and has built up to artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Benchmarks without context are meaningless: The pitfalls of data without insight
item.description: It was a typical conversation with a client when the question came up: “What’s the average security spend for organizations like ours?” I’ve heard variations of this query countless times.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Russia Spreads Misinformation on Hurricane Response - A Threat to US Democracy
item.description: Russia's efforts to manipulate American discourse before the presidential election, new research shows
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House reviewing HIPAA cybersecurity updates
item.description: HHS wants to boost the cybersecurity protections required under HIPAA, as ransomware groups continue to target sensitive healthcare information. The post White House reviewing HIPAA cybersecurity updates first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: DHS fosters intentional approach to AI for cybersecurity
item.description: AI is one of the most potent tools supplementing the cybersecurity efforts of federal agencies, but DHS is ensuring it's implemented responsibly and securely. The post DHS fosters intentional approach to AI for cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to prepare your online accounts for when you die
item.description: Most people have accumulated a pile of data on their social media and digital accounts over their lifetimes.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Deception in democracy: Beware the most common types of election-related scams
item.description: Cybersecurity risks are ramping up as the Nov. 5 U.S. general election approaches. Social media and the internet are used to spread propaganda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hong Kong discovers dinosaur fossils for the first time
item.description: Officials in Hong Kong say they have discovered dinosaur fossils in the city for the first time on a remote, uninhabited island that’s part of a geopark.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Crypto's impact on 2024 election will be 'fascinating to see': Mulvaney
item.description: NewsNation political contributor Mick Mulvaney said crypto donations in upcoming elections will be "fascinating.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, The Hill

item.title: Jet-tracker who watched Zuckerberg, Trump, Musk says Meta has banned his accounts
item.description: Meta has removed accounts that track the plane movements of its CEO Mark Zuckerberg, X owner Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos, Kim Kardashian, former President Trump and others, account owner Jack Sweeney said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: BRIGANTINE, N.J. (AP) — Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents escalate their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The clever tech powering a wave of pig-butchering scams
item.description: Pig-butchering scams are all over social media platforms and are becoming more sophisticated by the day; here's how to spot the latest tricks so you don't become the next victim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Agencies prep for next phase of ‘zero trust’ cyber upgrades
item.description: An upcoming deadline will provide a pulse check on the government-wide push toward "zero trust" cybersecurity defenses. The post Agencies prep for next phase of ‘zero trust’ cyber upgrades first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: AI dominates Gartner’s 2025 predictions
item.description: Artificial Intelligence continues to dominate this week’s Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo, as well as the research firm’s annual predictions list.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tech giants press Congress to codify AI Safety Institute
item.description: A coalition of more than 60 tech companies and industry groups, including Amazon, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Microsoft and OpenAI, urged congressional leaders Tuesday to pass legislation permanently authorizing the U.S. Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute by the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Wall Street Journal, New York Post sue AI startup
item.description: Media conglomerate News Corp, which owns The Wall Street Journal, New York Post and a number of other media properties, is suing artificial intelligence (AI) startup Perplexity over what it alleges is copyright infringement.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Floating energy platform could change way you get power in future
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how NoviOcean's hybrid platform uses wind, solar and wave power to generate clean energy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Buyer’s guide: How to choose between Microsoft 365 and Office 2024
item.description: Microsoft Office — best known for its Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook productivity applications — is how billions of people around the world work and study, whether they do it from home, an office, a classroom, or a combination of those.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Stopping the rot in AI spending
item.description: They say that half the money spent on advertising is wasted, but the problem is figuring out which half. If that’s true, the problem is arguably worse for AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Over 600M cyberattacks target Windows users every day
item.description: Windows operating system users face more than 600 million cyberattacks every day, according to Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson. The attacks are on the rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, Fox 5
